mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in initial jobless claims (PAYEMS decline) signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in recession risks.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or PAYEMS declines correlate positively with returns (Pearson r > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust economic demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in co-expansionary periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term between GDPC1 and HOUST shows negative coefficient in regression.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL above 3%) combined with stagnant monetary policy (FEDFUNDS < 1%) creates a 'stagflation' risk scenario, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as real rates turn negative.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows positive coefficient in regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate corporate profit declines.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or INDPRO declines correlate positively with returns (Pearson r > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% combined with rising wage growth (PAYEMS) indicates labor market tightness, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through inflationary pressure and corporate pricing power.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5% and PAYEMS rises > 100k in 1 month
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in tight labor scenarios are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term between UNRATE and PAYEMS shows negative coefficient in regression.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in residential construction (RSAFS) indicates strong household demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in < 40% of triggered instances, or RSAFS increases correlate negatively with returns (Pearson r < -0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) during a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 4%) indicates over-tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as real economic activity contracts.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4% and INDPRO falls > 1.5% in 1 month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening-supply scenarios are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term between INDPRO and FEDFUNDS shows positive coefficient in regression.
